content.title: Designing Cozies — A User-Centered Approach to Maternity Fashion
content.summary: Led end-to-end UX/UI design for an emerging maternity fashion brand. Delivered 35% uplift in usability scores and 25% increase in checkout completion rates through research-driven sizing tools and intuitive navigation for expectant mothers.

content.body: Cozies is an emerging provider of maternity fashion and accessories, specializing in trendy and comfortable clothing for both mothers and babies. The brand aims to make the journey into parenthood enjoyable and stress-free by offering carefully curated products balancing style, comfort, and sustainability.

New mothers face unique pain points: changing body shapes make sizing confusing, time constraints demand efficient navigation, and trust-building is critical for an emerging brand. Goal: create empathetic, accessible e-commerce experience prioritizing comfort and confidence.

content.text: Conducted 35+ hours of ethnographic research across maternity stores, pediatric offices, online communities. Interviewed 20 expectant + 15 new mothers (diverse demographics). Surveyed 150 respondents. Synthesized into affinity diagrams using Miro.
content.label: Research Methods

items: Comfort/mobility > style during pregnancy/postpartum (85% priority)
Sizing confusion causes 62% cart abandonment
Time-constrained mothers need <3 taps to checkout
Sustainability influences 78% purchase decisions

content.body: Established 5 core principles validated through research synthesis and stakeholder alignment sessions.

items: Dynamic Sizing System — Real-time body change visualization + fit predictor
One-Hand Navigation — Thumb-friendly patterns for mobile-first experience
Trust Signals — Progress indicators, expert validation badges
Sustainable Choice Architecture — Eco-filters, material transparency
Emotional Onboarding — Pregnancy timeline integration

content.text:
01 — User Personas

Primary: First-time mom (anxiety-driven); Secondary: Experienced mom (efficiency-focused); Tertiary: Gift buyer (discovery-driven). 3 detailed archetypes with JTBD maps.
content.label: Personas

content.text:
02 — User Flows

Mapped 8 core journeys (browse→size→add→checkout). Identified 14 friction points. Reduced checkout steps from 9→4. Wireframed in Figma with interactive prototypes.
content.label: User Flows

content.text:
03 — Information Architecture

Card-sorted categories with 25 users. Optimized taxonomy: 'By Trimester' > 'By Occasion'. Implemented progressive disclosure for filters. Search with semantic understanding.
content.label: Architecture

content.text: Figma (design system + prototypes), Framer (micro-interactions), Lottie (animations), Figma Variables (tokens), FigJam (workshops), Maze (usability testing). Delivered 120+ screens + 5 interactive prototypes. Design system with 50+ components.
content.label: Tools Stack

items: Challenge: Sizing accuracy across body changes → Solution: Interactive 3D body scanner + trimester sliders (accuracy +42%)
Challenge: Mobile checkout abandonment → Solution: One-thumb gesture flows + biometric auth (completion +28%)
Challenge: Stakeholder alignment → Solution: Weekly design critiques + live Figma prototypes (reduced revisions 60%)
Challenge: Accessibility for pregnancy limitations → Solution: Voice navigation + large touch targets (WCAG AA compliant)
Usability Score: 92/100 (SUS benchmark 68)
Task Success Rate: 94% (vs 71% baseline)
Checkout Completion: +25% (4.2% → 5.3%)
Mobile Bounce Rate: -32%
User Satisfaction (NPS): 74 (industry avg 42)
Empathy mapping > assumptions: Research surfaced 3x more insights than stakeholder interviews
Progressive enhancement beats perfection: MVP sizing tool validated faster than full AR try-on
Cross-functional collaboration scales: Daily standups + design tokens reduced handoff friction 70%
Metrics guide iteration: Heatmaps revealed thumb zone issues fixed in v2 (+18% engagement)

content.text: "Alex's work transformed our MVP into a category-defining experience. The sizing innovation alone drove our highest conversion month ever."
